claim: The study evaluates modern lightweight time-series approaches that are appropriate for Empatica wearable data and weak onset-only seizure labels.

methods.result: Standard AUC 0.4259, events 1/6; Pro AUC 0.5485, events 3/6

methods.implemented_as: Validation-selected refractory interval over detector scores
methods.result: Standard alert FA/hr 0.82; Pro alert FA/hr 0.91

methods.implemented_as: Repeated seed and label-duration sensitivity studies

ttm.title: Tiny Time Mixers, NeurIPS 2024
ttm.relevance: Supports compact patch-mixer and channel-independent time-series modeling under edge constraints.

wearable_armband.title: Wearable armband seizure detection with ACC and PPG, 2025
wearable_armband.relevance: Supports multimodal wearable detection and two-step candidate screening/reporting with false alarms.

eegformer.title: Compact transformer false-alarm reduction for wearable seizure detection
eegformer.relevance: Supports compact transformer comparison, while noting that EEG datasets are not directly comparable to Empatica non-EEG weak labels.

wearable_review.title: Systematic review of non-invasive wearable seizure detection
wearable_review.relevance: Supports reporting sensitivity and false-alarm burden instead of raw accuracy.

not_directly_comparable.method: EEG-specific seizure transformers trained on CHB-MIT or wearable EEG
not_directly_comparable.reason: Those studies use EEG morphology and often more precise event boundaries; this project uses Empatica ACC/PPG/EDA/TEMP with onset-only labels.
Large pretrained models are not aligned with the TinyML/edge memory target and would not solve onset-only label uncertainty.
